hypothesis: If a generative model for Covalent Triazine Frameworks is pre-trained on large chemical databases (ChEMBL and ZINC-15) and fine-tuned on a curated CTF dataset with reinforcement learning using expert-defined rewards, then it will produce CTF candidates with higher validity, novelty, and diversity and better adherence to expert criteria than models that lack such transfer learning and reward guidance, because the transfer learning and reward signals calibrate the model toward the CTF-relevant chemical space and desired properties.
